title: Parkio Daily for June 9, 2026
teaser: Disneyland drops its 11am park hopping rule today; La Poutinerie opens June 13 at EPCOT; Magic Kingdom food trucks now gone.

rides.name: Big Thunder Mountain Railroad
rides.note: Moat refill complete, Tomorrowland is quieter

rides.name: Rise of the Resistance
rides.note: Monstropolis construction noise is mid-morning only

breaking.title: Disneyland removes 11am park hopping restriction
breaking.body: Starting today, guests with Park Hopper tickets can switch between Disneyland and California Adventure at any time. The pandemic-era rule that held hoppers until 11am is officially gone.

parkioInsight.text: Rope-drop one park, then hop as early as 10am if crowds feel heavy. This flexibility tends to work best on days when Disneyland opens before California Adventure.

bignews.title: La Poutinerie debuts June 13 at EPCOT
bignews.body: The reimagined Canadian quick-service location opens Friday with poutine bowls, sandwiches, and desserts. Full menu and pricing have been released.

parkioInsight.text: Opening-week lines for new dining locations tend to peak between noon and 2pm. A late-afternoon stop around 4pm usually cuts your wait.

bignews.title: Magic Kingdom moat refilled after castle makeover
bignews.body: The water around Cinderella Castle has been restored, marking the unofficial end of the multi-month refurbishment. New landscaping and pathways are now visible.

parkioInsight.text: Photo spots around the hub tend to be least crowded during the first hour after rope drop and again after 8pm. Mid-afternoon sun creates harsh shadows.

bignews.title: Disney Springs food trucks permanently closed
bignews.body: Three mobile food vendors have been removed from Disney Springs. The trucks were a fixture near the parking garages for several years.

parkioInsight.text: Quick-bite options near the garages are now limited. Plan a stop at one of the indoor quick-service spots if you're short on time between shopping and dinner.

topstories.title: Kendi the rhino undergoes successful eye surgery
topstories.body: The 27-year-old white rhino at Animal Kingdom received treatment for cataracts and has regained her sight. Disney's animal care team caught the cloudiness early during routine observation.

parkioInsight.text: Guests on Kilimanjaro Safaris in the morning often see rhinos near the water feature. Ask a cast member at the loading area which vehicle rows offer the best sightlines.

topstories.title: Monstropolis construction progressing at Hollywood Studios
topstories.body: Steel frameworks are rising behind the walls where the Monsters Inc. land is taking shape. Disney released new details and images of the ongoing build.

parkioInsight.text: Construction noise near Sunset Boulevard tends to be loudest between 10am and 1pm. If you're planning a table-service lunch nearby, request a table away from the windows.

topstories.title: 27 Walt Disney World restaurant menus updated
topstories.body: Locations including Morimoto Asia and Sunshine Seasons rolled out menu changes this week. Pricing and dish offerings have shifted at quick-service and table-service spots across the resort.

parkioInsight.text: Menu refreshes often include limited-time items that disappear within a few weeks. If something catches your eye, try it early in your trip rather than saving it for the last day.

topstories.title: New chicken tender bowls debut at Magic Kingdom
topstories.body: A fresh snack lineup featuring tater tot and chicken tender combinations has launched at quick-service locations in the park and at the Grand Floridian.

parkioInsight.text: Quick-service counter lines tend to spike right before parades and fireworks. Ordering 30 minutes before showtime usually keeps your wait under 10 minutes.

topstories.title: Polynesian Resort construction reaches new milestone
topstories.body: The reimagined arrival experience, including rerouted bus lanes and vehicle entrances, is nearing completion. Work around the resort continues but major progress is visible.

parkioInsight.text: Bus drop-off and pickup areas may still have temporary signage and detours. Allow an extra 5 minutes when navigating the resort during your stay.

topstories.title: Toy Story 5 mystery pin set arrives at California Adventure
topstories.body: A new collectible pin series inspired by the upcoming film launched at the park. Each blind box features characters in social-media-style designs.

parkioInsight.text: Limited-edition pins tied to new releases often sell out at flagship stores within days. Check Elias & Co. near the main entrance first thing if you're collecting.

icymi.title: Donald and Daisy Duck appear at EPCOT Passholder event
icymi.body: The characters are scheduled for select appearances during the V.I.PASSHOLDER Summer Days celebration at EPCOT. The lounge inside Restaurant Marrakesh and exclusive merchandise are also part of the event.

icymi.title: Father's Day gift guide released by Disney
icymi.body: The official Disney Parks Blog shared a curated list of merchandise spanning Toy Story, Star Wars, and Marvel themes. Items are available online and in park shops.

icymi.title: Cool Kids Summer snacks under ten dollars announced
icymi.body: Disney highlighted budget-friendly treats available during the summer event at Walt Disney World. Options include sweets and savory bites across the parks.

icymi.title: Disneyland Halloween celebration details unveiled
icymi.body: The resort announced dates and offerings for the 2026 Halloween season, including ride overlays, themed merchandise, and seasonal food. The celebration begins later this summer.

spotlight.title: Bluey's Wild World settling into Animal Kingdom
spotlight.body: The new character experience launched May 26 and continues beyond summer. Guests can meet Bluey and Bingo and explore themed activities celebrating Australian wildlife.

parkioInsight.text: Character meet queues for new experiences often stretch longest between 11am and 3pm. Arriving right at park opening or after 5pm usually cuts your wait by half.
